mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad-based economic strength and improving corporate revenue prospects, leading to positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Job growth is a primary indicator of the business cycle phase.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 6-month change in PAYEMS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate at the zero lower bound, any significant increase signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle. This increases the discount rate for future earnings and causes a negative market reaction.
evidence_refs: fact:The market is anticipating the first rate hike since 2006.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has a positive average 20-day return in the periods immediately following the first two rate hikes of a new tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) and decelerating real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates stagflationary pressure. This compresses corporate profit margins and reduces consumer purchasing power, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was characterized by poor equity performance.
failure_condition: A regression model shows the interaction term between CPIAUCSL momentum and RSAFS momentum is not a statistically significant predictor (p > 0.10) of 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic recovery, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) confirms that the supply side is robustly responding to renewed demand. This signals strong corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 6%, positive 3-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid fall in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) suggests the labor market is tightening quickly. This can lead to wage-push inflation and prompt the Federal Reserve to adopt a more hawkish stance, which is negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed dual mandate includes maximum employment and stable prices.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) is a strong leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown, as residential investment has a large multiplier effect. This signals future weakness in corporate earnings and negatively impacts equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing downturns preceded the 2001 and 2008 recessions.
failure_condition: The 6-month momentum of HOUST fails to produce a rank information coefficient below -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is tight (low UNRATE) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating, it signals an overheating economy based on the Phillips Curve. This forces the central bank to tighten policy aggressively, causing a negative repricing of equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The Phillips Curve relationship is a key input for monetary policy.
failure_condition: During periods that meet the trigger condition, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A surprisingly strong monthly retail sales (RSAFS) print indicates robust consumer spending, causing an immediate upward revision to near-term GDP and corporate earnings forecasts, driving short-term positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Equity markets show measurable reactions to economic data surprises.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from the average return on non-trigger days (t-test p > 0.10).
